Name: perceptual_rate_distortion_compression
Title: Integrating Perceptual Fidelity into Rate-Distortion Model Compression
Short Hypothesis: Incorporating perceptual fidelity into the rate-distortion framework significantly enhances model compression efficiency without sacrificing predictive accuracy.
Related Work: While prior works such as 'Rate Distortion For Model Compression' and 'Rate–Distortion–Perception Trade-Off' touch upon rate-distortion theory, few fully integrate perceptual quality into the compression process. This proposal aims to bridge that gap by explicitly modeling perceptual fidelity alongside traditional accuracy metrics.
Abstract: As deep learning models grow in size and complexity, the need for efficient compression methods becomes critical. This proposal introduces a novel framework that integrates perceptual quality measures into rate-distortion optimization for model compression. By viewing model compression through a dual lens of task accuracy and perceptual fidelity, we aim to establish a theoretical foundation that not only minimizes the traditional distortion metrics but also considers the perceptual aspects of the model's output. We hypothesize that models compressed under this dual framework will achieve superior performance on downstream tasks while being more efficient in terms of memory and computational requirements. The proposed framework will yield quantitative predictions regarding achievable rates and distortion curves, which will be validated through a series of experiments.
Experiments: Develop a model compression algorithm that implements the proposed rate-distortion framework with perceptual quality constraints.
Evaluate the performance of the compressed models on benchmark datasets, comparing accuracy and perceptual metrics against state-of-the-art compression methods.
Quantitatively analyze the trade-offs between compression rate, accuracy, and perceptual fidelity, plotting the resulting curves against theoretical limits derived from the rate-distortion function.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the challenge of accurately measuring perceptual fidelity, which may vary across different applications. Additionally, balancing traditional accuracy with perceptual metrics may introduce complexity in the optimization process.
